notes: lambda_even here is the smallest-positive eigenvalue of the even-sector Galerkin matrix at c=100, N=150, dps=500 — an empirically distinguished positive branch, NOT a theorem-level ground-state identification. The raw finite-precision spectrum at this cell additionally contains 5 negative-sign eigenvalues at log10|e| in {-40.66, -59.75, -96.75, -165.15, -223.69}; these reproduce identically across dps=500 and dps=1000 (see results_c100_N150_T800_dps1000_v020.json), and we attribute them to condition-number-driven loss of positivity in the finite-N projection. Continuum positivity of QW_lambda for lambda > 1 is equivalent to the Riemann Hypothesis (Connes 2026 Sec 4.1) and is not assumed at lambda = sqrt(100). The smallest-positive eigenvector produces the gamma_1...gamma_10 extraction reported in c100_N150_dps1000_gamma_extraction.json (219-242 matching digits at dps=1000); the identification of this branch with the CCM 2025 Theorem 1.1 ground-state construction is conditional on a future certification of the negative-sign block.
